data.title: European Consumers Show Limited Interest In Digital Euro
data.description:
According to PANews, a recent report from the European Central Bank (ECB) highlights the low interest among European consumers in central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), raising concerns for the planned launch of the digital euro. The ECB's working paper, which surveyed approximately 19,000 respondents across 11 eurozone countries, underscores significant communication challenges in promoting the digital euro to European households.

The report reveals that when asked to hypothetically allocate €10,000 (approximately $10,800) across various assets, Europeans assigned only a small portion to the digital euro, indicating minimal impact on traditional liquid assets such as cash, current deposits, or savings accounts. Europeans exhibit a strong preference for existing payment methods and fail to perceive substantial benefits in new payment systems among numerous online and offline alternatives. The findings suggest that convincing some users of the added value of CBDCs may pose a challenge for policymakers, necessitating further research in this area.

The study indicates that while the introduction of the digital euro is unlikely to significantly affect financial stability, its adoption faces considerable obstacles due to consumer habits. It emphasizes the importance of targeted communication to address ongoing consumer resistance to the digital euro. The research also finds that European consumers are interested in video-based education and training, concluding that educating the public with video information related to CBDCs could facilitate broader adoption of the digital euro.

data.title: Brevan Howard Reduces Risk Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to PANews, Brevan Howard Asset Management, a prominent U.S. macro hedge fund, is significantly reducing the risk exposure of its traders following a decline in performance and the evaporation of last year's gains. Informed sources indicate that CEO Aron Landy has implemented defensive measures in response to increased market volatility and turmoil, including lowering risk limits for some traders. An investor letter reveals that Brevan Howard's flagship Master Fund fell by 1% in the first week of March, extending its year-to-date loss to 5.4%, compared to a 5.1% gain last year. Another major fund, Alpha Strategies, declined by 0.8% during the same period but has risen 1.5% this year. Since its inception in 2003, the flagship fund has only experienced an annual loss exceeding 5% once.

In recent weeks, global market volatility has intensified amid conflicts between U.S. President Donald Trump's administration and global trade partners, raising investor concerns over issues ranging from tariffs on Canadian goods to increased defense spending in Europe. This turbulence has also affected other hedge fund giants such as Citadel, Millennium Management, and DE Shaw & Co.

data.title: Deribit: About $3.27 billion in crypto options will expire this Friday, and BTC's biggest pain point is $86,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Deribit data, about $3.27 billion of crypto options will expire at 08:00 UTC this Friday (March 15), with Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) dominating.
BTC option open interest notional value: $2.87 billion | Maximum pain point: $86,000 | Put/call ratio: 0.74
ETH option open interest notional value: $399 million | Maximum pain point: $2,150 | Put/call ratio: 0.71
Deribit pointed out that the market's maximum pain point (Max Pain) has been moving down for several consecutive weeks.

data.title: Zhu Su Highlights Challenges Facing Ethereum Amid CeDeFi Rise
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on March 13, Zhu Su, the founder of Three Arrows Capital, expressed concerns on social media regarding the impact of CeDeFi on Ethereum. He stated that the rise of CeDeFi represents a significant challenge for Ethereum, as many open smart contracts, initially envisioned to operate on distributed networks, are now being executed on single servers with closed-source code. Zhu Su emphasized the need for Ethereum to embrace applications that require global distributed computing and censorship resistance, particularly those with cryptographic attributes like privacy protection. He warned that if Ethereum's performance remains subpar, the revival of DeFi will be difficult to achieve. In a world where validator decentralization and smart contract demand are low, off-chain solutions may become mainstream due to their cost and efficiency advantages.

data.title: The Ethermine-related wallet, which has been silent for 3 years, withdrew 2,692 ETH from OKX, about 5.01 million US dollars
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of Spot On Chain, an on-chain analyst, an Ethermine-related wallet created 7 years ago became active again after 3 years of silence, withdrawing 2,692 ETH from OKX, about 5.01 million US dollars.
The wallet had been partially dormant for a long time, and it is unclear where its subsequent funds flowed.
